headlines.title: Nominee for top intel post faces lawmakers eager to see him replace the current leader
headlines.description: Jay Clayton’s confirmation hearing comes amid growing concern among Democrats that the Trump administration will try to use the intelligence agencies to interfere with the midterm elections.

headlines.title: Blanche says he will work towards 'restoring American trust' in the Justice Department
headlines.description: During his opening statement at a confirmation hearing, acting Attorney General Todd Blanche discussed his efforts to combat crime and enforce Justice Department policies while "restoring American trust" in the agency.

headlines.title: Acting AG Todd Blanche Faces High-Stakes Confirmation Hearing
headlines.description: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche, President Donald Trump’s pick to lead the Justice Department, is set to be grilled by lawmakers on Capitol Hill during his confirmation hearing. He’s also facing a fierce campaign to block his nomination from survivors of Jeffrey Epstein. NBC’s Hallie Jackson reports for TODAY.

headlines.title: House Passes Bill to Make Daylight Saving Time Permanent
headlines.description: The House overwhelmingly passed a bill called the Sunshine Protection Act on Tuesday to make daylight saving time permanent. The legislation now heads to the Senate where, if passed, it would keep clocks at their current setting year-round.

headlines.title: State Assemblyman Alex Bores speaks on AI and politics
headlines.description: State Assemblyman Alex Bores, a candidate for New York's 12th Congressional District, speaks with NBC News’ Gadi Schwartz about why some top artificial intelligence companies are opposing his candidacy and whether AI should play a role in politics.

headlines.title: ICE and the Senate react to the latest shootings: From the Politics Desk
headlines.description: Welcome to From the Politics Desk, a daily newsletter that brings you the NBC News Politics team’s latest reporting and analysis from the White House, Capitol Hill and the campaign trail.

headlines.title: House passes Trump-backed bill that would make daylight saving time permanent
headlines.description: The legislation, known as the Sunshine Protection Act and backed by President Donald Trump, now heads to the Senate for consideration.

headlines.title: Justice Barrett recounts swatting incident in testimony before Congress
headlines.description: Supreme Court justices are testifying before Congress for the first time in seven years, seeking additional funds to combat a rise in security threats.

headlines.title: Trump says FBI is ‘wasting their time’ investigating Lindsey Graham’s death
headlines.description: The president told reporters that he was aware of conspiracy theories circulating about the sudden death of his friend and ally.

headlines.title: Darline Graham is sworn in as senator to fill brother Lindsey Graham’s seat
headlines.description: Darline Graham, previously not a politician or a public figure, is expected to be a reliable vote for the Republican majority.

headlines.title: E. Jean Carroll collects more than $5M from Trump in sexual abuse and defamation case
headlines.description: Attorneys for the president had tried to block the money from being turned over after a jury three years ago found him liable.

headlines.title: How Lindsey Graham’s death quickly changes a busy Senate: From the Politics Desk
headlines.description: Welcome to From the Politics Desk, a daily newsletter that brings you the NBC News Politics team’s latest reporting and analysis from the White House, Capitol Hill and the campaign trail.
